candidate_scope: Add an under-1-minute fast lane and reorganize ordinary PR/verify toward a 5-minute layered gate using existing impact-report/change-policy/quality-gate architecture.

must_preserve_ledger.claim: Make this project's tests lighter and faster while preserving high-quality signal.
First scope adds an under-1-minute fast lane and reorganizes ordinary PR/verify toward a 5-minute target.
Preferred technical direction is Option B: Layered PR Gate using existing impact-report/change-policy/quality-gate architecture.
Fast-lane evidence should be changed-area high-signal unit tests plus a tiny fixed core smoke set.
Preserve bun run verify / quality:pr as the PR readiness entrypoint.
E2E, live-provider, and release-gate evidence should be conditional for ordinary PR/verify, not removed.
High quality means critical-path regression protection, low flaky rate, and changed-area coverage.
Do not weaken coverage baselines, thresholds, same-area test policy, persistence checks, quarantine governance, or release confidence without explicit approval.
Existing source-grounded surfaces include package.json, scripts/quality-gate/modes.ts, runner.ts, coverage.ts, scripts/pr/impact-report.ts, and scripts/pr/change-policy.ts.
Fast-lane success is a development signal, not full PR readiness.
Expansion-ready test impact graph is deferred; start with layered PR gate first.
Exact full-coverage handling under the 5-minute PR target is a soft planning question.
